reasoning: The WGS1YR yield currently sits near 3.69%-3.70% (based on daily DGS1 readings in early April 2026). Short-term linear trends suggest a slight downward momentum over the very near term (1 week), anchoring my initial 2026-04-19 prediction at 0.47. However, macro evidence indicates a shift in market sentiment: recent strong Treasury supply and inflation fears have pushed fed funds futures to price in a >50% probability of a rate hike by late 2026. Such pricing shifts typically place upward pressure on the 1-year Treasury yield. Thus, for the 1-month to 6-month horizons, I forecast an increased probability (peaking at 0.56 for October 2026) that the yield will be higher than the mid-April baseline, tracking the heightened rate hike anticipation. For timeframes beyond 1 year, uncertainty dramatically escalates due to unpredictable macroeconomic cycles, possible reversions to a lower neutral rate, and term premium fluctuations. Reflecting this rising uncertainty, my probabilities smoothly decay toward the 0.50 anchor for the distant 3-year, 5-year, and 10-year horizons.
